intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable containers over stark rectangular layouts
facade.axisTargeted: Warmth vs sterility in data presentation
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace organic shapes and subtle shadows; prioritize human comfort in clinical contexts
facade.rejectImplies: Sharp edges and minimal decoration align better with the precision/authority health data demands

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, lightweight prompts over clinical data entry forms
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Build voice around natural language (e.g., 'How's your sleep been?' instead of 'Sleep duration (hours)'). Use encouraging tone in feedback.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into structured, objective interface. Neutral language. Data-first presentation.

evidence.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable containers over stark rectangular layouts
evidence.implication: Embrace organic shapes and subtle shadows; prioritize human comfort in clinical contexts

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers a gentle, low-stress visual language that feels caring and non-clinical, even when displaying health data
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, organic vs digital
facade.label: Soft curves, muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: Move toward rounded interfaces, earth tones, soft shadows, and a handmade aesthetic that humanizes data
facade.rejectImplies: The user may prefer crisp, modern clarity or a more structured/technical appearance—lean into clean lines and neutral palette instead

facade.hypothesis: Fluid, animated transitions between dashboard views will feel more engaging and cohesive than instant page loads
facade.label: Smooth slide transitions
facade.acceptImplies: User values motion and continuity; dashboard should prioritize flowing transitions between experiment views, charts, and detail panels
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers snappy, instant feedback; transitions should be removed or minimized in favor of immediate state changes

facade.hypothesis: Users prioritize seeing summary metrics (weight, blood pressure, sleep hours) with directional arrows over detailed charts for quick health status checks
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data (tables, raw numbers) vs Key metrics (summary cards with visual indicators)
facade.label: Big numbers with trends
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard leads with large, scannable KPI cards showing current value + weekly/monthly trend. Charts are secondary.
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard prioritizes detailed charts and time-series visualization over headline numbers. Data density is the main view.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left navigation with health metrics displayed as vertically scrollable cards, reducing context switching
facade.axisTargeted: Layout architecture (sidebar vs. tabs vs. hybrid)
facade.label: Sidebar + card stack
facade.acceptImplies: Future probes should explore card-based breakdowns, persistent navigation benefits, and vertical scrolling patterns for experiment data
facade.rejectImplies: Explore top-nav or tab-based structures, possibly with modal or panel-based detail views instead

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer soft, warm illumination in health dashboards to reduce screen fatigue and create a calming atmosphere during frequent check-ins
facade.axisTargeted: Light temperature and intensity (warm vs cool, bright vs dim)
facade.label: Warm glow lighting
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize amber/warm color temperatures, subtle gradients, and low-contrast backgrounds to ease daily engagement with health data
facade.rejectImplies: Users may tolerate or prefer clinical clarity—cooler tones, high contrast, and bright whites—suggesting the interface should feel more analytical than soothing

draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.

draft.nextHint: User accepted soft rounded cards, signaling preference for warm, human-centered design over clinical aesthetics. Next probes should explore: data density (all metrics at once vs. progressive disclosure), tone (motivational vs. neutral), and feedback style (celebratory vs. calm). Consider testing a variant with more narrative insight or a variant with gamified progress elements.

data.hint: User accepted soft rounded cards, signaling preference for warm, human-centered design over clinical aesthetics. Consider testing a variant with more narrative insight or a variant with gamified progress elements.
